name: n8n: Dockerized self-host stack
description: Self-hosted n8n automation layout with Docker Compose, backup scripts, and proxy-oriented folders—opinionated structure for secrets, network exposure, and repeatable operations on private infrastructure.
longDescription: Self-hosted workflow automation using Docker Compose: service definitions, container context, scripts, and supporting static assets to run n8n on private infrastructure. Backup helpers and deployment-oriented directories are structured so secrets, TLS material, and internal endpoints stay out of public trees and are loaded only from private configuration. The layout emphasises repeatable operations—start/stop, backups, upgrades—and keeps business-specific workflow logic out of versioned files where it would couple IP to infrastructure.
features: Compose-first layout for bringing automation online in a controlled environment
Backup script scaffolding for database snapshots and restore discipline
Supporting folders for proxy TLS material and maintenance pages (populated per operator)
Separation between runtime volumes, versioned configuration, and secret-bearing env files
Network posture documented: prefer reverse proxy + firewall over exposing node ports broadly
highlights: Keeps workflow automation under your own Docker runtime
Sensitive hostnames and secrets stay in private configuration, not in public-facing descriptions
challenges: Self-hosted automation must stay patched, backed up, and reachable only to trusted networks.
solutions: Rely on compose with documented volumes, scripted backups, and TLS termination at the edge—never embedding secrets in public JSON.
security: Store webhook URLs, API keys, and DB passwords only in private env files—never in public repos
Restrict administrative ports via firewall or VPN; terminate TLS at the edge proxy
Rotate automation credentials on a schedule; review n8n and image updates as supply-chain hygiene
Volume backups encrypted at rest when stored off-server
Principle of least privilege for service accounts reaching webhooks and databases
